system_context.prepended_by_claude_code: You are a Claude agent, built on Anthropic's Claude Agent SDK.
system_context.date_injection: system-reminder with current date injected into user message

eleven_twenty_game.description: Direct prediction of human distribution on Arad & Rubinstein (2012) 11-20 Money Request Game

unnamed_b20.prompt: Game rules only, bonus=20, no paper citation
unnamed_b20.notes: Model cited Arad & Rubinstein (2012) unprompted. Predicted peak at 20 (35%), humans peak at 17 (32%).

weak_bonus_b10.prompt: Same structure, bonus=10 (no published human data)
weak_bonus_b10.notes: Model cited A&R despite different parameters. 60% at 19-20.

strong_bonus_b50.prompt: Same structure, bonus=50 (no published human data)
strong_bonus_b50.notes: Model cited A&R. 70% at 19-20. Shifted WRONG direction (more mass at top with stronger undercutting incentive).

named_b20.prompt: Same rules + explicit paper citation (Arad & Rubinstein 2012, AER)
named_b20.notes: Named condition slightly worse than unnamed. 64% at 19-20.

full_system_b20.prompt: Default system prompt, tools enabled, read prompt from file

charness_rabin_games.description: Predictions on 20 two-stage dictator games from Charness & Rabin (2002), data verified from Manning & Horton Table D2
charness_rabin_games.method: All 20 games in single batched prompt
key_observations: Model tends to predict proportions closer to 0.5 than actual human data (regression to mean)
8/20 Player B predictions are exactly 0.62 — suggests default heuristic, not per-game reasoning
Model misses extreme human behavior badly (Barc7 B: predicted 0.48, actual 0.06; Barc9 B: predicted 0.62, actual 0.94)
But gets direction right most of the time (r=0.73 for A, r=0.83 for B)
Despite Charness-Rabin (2002) being famous (5000+ citations), model doesn't seem to have memorized game-specific proportions
